name: NBA Teams
description: NBA franchises, cities, and basketball history

questions.q: Which NBA team plays its home games in Chicago?

source.notes: Joined the NBA in 1966; won six titles in the Michael Jordan era.

questions.q: Which NBA team calls Boston home and plays its games in the Eastern Conference?

source.notes: Founded 1946; a charter member of the NBA.

questions.q: Which NBA team plays in Miami, named for the city's sweltering climate?

source.notes: Entered the NBA as an expansion team in 1988.

questions.q: Which Canadian city is home to the NBA's only non-U.S. franchise, the Raptors?

source.notes: Founded 1995; won the NBA championship in 2019.

questions.q: Which NBA team shares its home city's nickname with the Hollywood film industry and wears gold-and-purple?

source.notes: Originally the Minneapolis Lakers; moved to LA in 1960.

questions.q: Which NBA team is based in Dallas and takes its name from an independent-minded cowboy?

source.notes: Joined the NBA as an expansion team in 1980.

questions.q: Which NBA team plays its home games at Madison Square Garden in New York City?

source.notes: Short for 'Knickerbockers'; founded 1946.

questions.q: What jersey number did Michael Jordan famously wear during most of his career with the Chicago Bulls?

source.notes: Jordan also briefly wore 45 after his return from baseball in 1995.

questions.q: Which NBA team plays in San Antonio and is named for a cowboy's riding accessory?

source.notes: Joined the NBA from the ABA in 1976; five championships since 1999.

questions.q: Which NBA team is based in Salt Lake City and kept its music-themed name after relocating from New Orleans?

source.notes: Founded as the New Orleans Jazz in 1974; moved to Utah in 1979.

questions.q: Which NBA team is based in Philadelphia and named after the year of American independence?

source.notes: Originally the Syracuse Nationals; renamed after moving to Philadelphia in 1963.

questions.q: Oklahoma City's NBA team uses which weather-themed name?

source.notes: Franchise relocated from Seattle (SuperSonics) in 2008.

questions.q: Which NBA team represents the San Francisco Bay Area under the name 'Golden State'?

source.notes: The 'Golden State' reference dates to the 1971–72 season.

questions.q: Which NBA team plays in Portland, Oregon?

source.notes: Founded 1970; won an NBA title in 1977.

questions.q: The NBA's Milwaukee franchise takes its name from which hoofed animal?

source.notes: Founded 1968; nicknamed after the male deer common to Wisconsin forests.

questions.q: Kobe Bryant's fierce on-court persona earned him which animal nickname?

source.notes: Bryant chose the nickname himself around 2003; inspired by the Kill Bill snake.

questions.q: The NBA's 1992 Olympic 'Dream Team' first made its mark at the Games in which city?

source.notes: Team USA won gold at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics.

questions.q: When Dr. James Naismith invented basketball in 1891, what did he use as the original hoops?

source.notes: Baskets were nailed to the balcony at a YMCA in Springfield, Massachusetts.

questions.q: Which Philadelphia Warrior scored a record 100 points in a single NBA game in 1962?

source.notes: Against the New York Knicks on March 2, 1962 in Hershey, Pennsylvania.

questions.q: James Naismith, the inventor of basketball, was born in which country?

source.notes: Born 1861 in Almonte, Ontario; invented the game in Massachusetts in 1891.

questions.q: Before relocating to Brooklyn in 2012, the NBA's Nets franchise was based in which state?

source.notes: The Nets played in New Jersey (most recently in Newark) from 1977 until 2012.

questions.q: Which NBA team was known as the Charlotte Bobcats before reclaiming an older insect-themed name in 2014?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: hornets are insects (not birds); the original name 'Charlotte Hornets' was used 1988–2002 before the team moved to New Orleans.
